videos.title: Top View, Side View & Front View of Objects | 3D Shapes for Kids
videos.description: Learn how everyday objects look different from the top, side, and front. Discover that a cup looks like a circle from above but like a rectangle from the side!
learningObjectives: Understand what top, side, and front views mean
Identify different views of common objects
keyConceptsCovered: Side view = looking from the side
Front view = looking straight at the object

videos.title: Different Views of 3D Shapes | How Objects Look from Different Angles
videos.description: Explore how 3D shapes like cubes, cylinders, cones, and pyramids look different depending on whether you view them from the top, side, or front.
learningObjectives: Match 3D shapes to their different views
keyConceptsCovered: Cylinder: circle from top, rectangle from side
Cone: circle from top, triangle from side

videos.title: Where Are You Hiding? | Seeing Objects from Different Positions
videos.description: Practice identifying which view of an object is shown. Is it the top view, side view, or front view? Play the guessing game!
keyConceptsCovered: Book: rectangle from top and front
Glass: circle from top, rectangle from side

videos.title: Practice: Match the View to the Object | Fun Quiz
videos.description: Test yourself! Can you match each view (top, side, front) to the correct object? A fun quiz-style video to check your understanding.

videos.title: Drawing Top, Side, and Front Views | Let's Draw Together
videos.description: Learn to draw the top view, side view, and front view of simple objects like a bottle, a box, and a ball. Grab your pencil and draw along!
keyConceptsCovered: Drawing top view as bird's eye view
Drawing side and front views accurately

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Fast-track through views with drawing challenge

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of views with practice

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Core views concepts after prerequisite review

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: When you look at a cylinder (like a tin can) from the top, what shape do you see?
questions.explanation: A cylinder looks like a circle when viewed from the top because its top face is circular.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Bring real objects to class (cups, boxes, balls) and let students observe them from different positions. Videos 1-3 are essential for all students.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child look at everyday objects from different angles at home. Ask 'What shape do you see from the top?'
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Try looking at objects around you from different positions. Can you draw what you see?
